mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 5.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profits and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in periods with declining INDPRO are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening economy that increases corporate profits and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with increasing GDPC1 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the housing market index above 1500 signals a strengthening housing market that increases consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1500 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 3-month increase in the housing market index signal a monetary easing cycle and a strengthening housing market that increases consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with both declining FEDFUNDS and increasing HOUST are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% and a 3-month decline in industrial production signal a weakening labor market and a weakening supply chain that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with both UNRATE > 5.5% and INDPRO < 100 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP and a 3-month increase in the housing market index signal a strengthening economy and a strengthening housing market that increases consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with both increasing GDPC1 and increasing HOUST are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
